methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Emerald AI is a Seed-stage climate and energy technology company founded by Varun Sivaram that has raised $24.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage companies (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M), supplemented by reference to a broader frontier-tech comp universe spanning one public company and eight private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using total capital raised as a proxy for current value, applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $61M. The peer-median approach anchors to the $150M median valuation across the three comparable companies, with a range of $110M to $200M. Triangulating these two methods produces a low estimate of $52M, median of $106M, and high of $172M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $52M to $172M, with a median implied value near $106M. Key uncertainties include the stage premium assignment—whether Emerald AI's Seed stage fully justifies a 2.5x step-up comparable to later-stage peers, and the representativeness of the three-company peer sample for a frontier-tech subsector where comparable transactions remain limited.
